channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:
1. Windows and Xbox rediscover user feedback and fans are wondering what took so long  Windows Central

  4. Mary Jo Foley: Microsoft Windows’ new lease on life — why now?  GeekWire
  5. Microsoft’s Windows 11 fix list for the year: 18 changes promised  PCWorld

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra is a major downgrade to S25 Ultra in one key area, and that's why I didn’t buy it - Android Police

item.title: Ubisoft's Insane Action Game Morbid Metal Hits Early Access, and We're Desperate to Play It on PS5 - Push Square
item.description:
1. Ubisoft's Insane Action Game Morbid Metal Hits Early Access, and We're Desperate to Play It on PS5  Push Square

item.title: Tomodachi Life Leaks: Early Copies of Living the Dream Reportedly Circulating - Sims Community
item.description:
1. Tomodachi Life Leaks: Early Copies of Living the Dream Reportedly Circulating  Sims Community
  2. A free demo for Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is out now!  nintendo.com
  3. Nintendo says Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream won't support the Switch 2's new resolution boost feature because the game runs in 'higher resolution' 1080p 'regardless of whether Handheld Mode Boost is enabled or disabled'  TechRadar
  4. Tomodachi Life doesn’t support Switch 2’s ‘handheld mode boost’ because it runs at 1080p in handheld, Nintendo says  Video Games Chronicle
  5. New Tomodachi Life: Living The Dream Trailer Continues Hugh Morris' Rejected Romance Drama  Nintendo Life

item.title: Microsoft denies Copilot is only for entertainment purpose, after its own document says do not trust AI - Windows Latest
item.description:
1. Microsoft denies Copilot is only for entertainment purpose, after its own document says do not trust AI  Windows Latest
  2. Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use  TechCrunch
  3. Even AI Companies Know Their Models Can’t Be Trusted  Medium
  4. Microsoft says Copilot is for entertainment purposes only, not serious use — firm pushing AI hard to consumers and businesses tells users not to rely on it for important advice  Tom's Hardware
  5. Copilot Terms Claim Microsoft's AI Is for 'Entertainment Purposes Only'  PCMag
